extName.description: Extension name shown in the store and browser.

extDesc.message: Brings likes, dislikes and the like/dislike ratio back to the YouTube Studio Content tab — from your own session only.

columnHeader.description: Header of the injected table column.

columnLoading.description: Tooltip while dislikes are not loaded yet.

columnLoadingSubs.description: Tooltip while the subscriber count is not loaded yet.

subsTooltip.description: Tooltip explanation of the subscriber number.

footerHint.message: Shows data from your own signed-in channel only. Reload the Content tab if nothing appears.

popupDebugActiveHint.message: Reload the Content tab, open the console (F12) and filter for [YTSR]. Then send me the output via "Report an issue".

welcomeActiveBody.message: The extension is active automatically right after installation. You don't need to enable anything — just open YouTube Studio.

welcomeWhereBody.message: Go to YouTube Studio and open the Content tab (the video list). Two extra columns are added on the right: "Likes (vs. Dislikes)" and "Subscribers (total)". The values load within a moment — reload the tab if nothing shows up.

welcomeToggleBody.message: Click the extension icon in the browser toolbar to open the settings popup. There you can toggle the display on/off. It is on by default. Tip: pin the icon so it's always visible.

welcomeDebugBody.message: YouTube sometimes changes its internal data without notice. If a column stays empty, the Debug mode helps me fix it:

welcomeDebugStep1.message: Click the extension icon and switch on Debug mode.

welcomeDebugStep2.message: Reload the Content tab and open the developer console with F12.

welcomeDebugStep4.message: Copy the output and send it to me via the GitHub issue tracker.

welcomePrivacyBody.message: Everything runs inside your own signed-in session. The extension shows only your own channel's numbers and sends nothing to any server or third party.

welcomeFooter.message: You can close this tab — the extension is ready to use.
